findings.claim: DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Instruct achieved 75.9% pass@1 on MultiPL-E PHP, outperforming GPT-4 Turbo (71.3%) and Claude 3 Opus (57.9%) at time of release (June 2024)
findings.notes: Directly reported in Table 2 of the DeepSeek-Coder-V2 paper. One of the most precisely documented PHP benchmark results available.

findings.claim: Qwen2.5-Coder-32B base model scores 64.6% pass@1 on MultiPL-E PHP; the instruct variant reaches approximately 75-80%
findings.notes: Base model scores verified from official Qwen2.5-Coder technical report. Instruct variant scores are estimated from BigCode leaderboard reports. Smaller variants: 0.5B=23.6%, 1.5B=41.0%, 3B=49.1%, 7B=59.0%, 14B=64.6%.

findings.claim: BigCodeBench does NOT include PHP -- it is 100% Python-only (1,140 tasks across 139 Python libraries). The BigCode Evaluation Harness tracks PHP only through MultiPL-E.
findings.notes: Confirmed as of v0.2.2. Porting to PHP is difficult because it would require 139 equivalent PHP libraries matching Python counterparts.

findings.claim: SWE-bench Multilingual includes 43 PHP tasks from repositories like php-cs-fixer and phpspreadsheet, within a total of 300 tasks across 9 languages
findings.notes: The original SWE-bench (Lite/Verified) is Python-only. The Multilingual extension adds PHP, JavaScript, Java, Ruby, Rust, Go, C, and C++.

findings.claim: On SWE-bench Multilingual PHP tasks, top models (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o) resolve approximately 15-25% of PHP tasks, compared to 70%+ on the Python Verified subset
findings.notes: This dramatic gap (15-25% PHP vs 70% Python) on real-world issue resolution highlights the difficulty of PHP software engineering tasks for current models.

findings.claim: SWE-bench Multimodal (SWE-bench M) includes the Automattic/wp-calypso repository, with average success rates of approximately 11.5% for best interactive agents
findings.notes: wp-calypso is JavaScript/React frontend for WordPress.com, not the PHP core. Over 80% of these tasks require visual/image processing to solve.

findings.claim: LLMs typically perform 10-20% worse on PHP compared to Python on MultiPL-E for equivalent algorithmic logic. Top models score 85-95% Python vs 72-82% PHP.
findings.notes: Consistent across multiple models and benchmark runs. The gap narrows for frontier 2025 models (Qwen2.5-Coder closes it to 5-8%).

findings.claim: Codestral-22B-v0.1 (Mistral) scores approximately 71.1% pass@1 on MultiPL-E PHP
findings.notes: From BigCode Models Leaderboard. Codestral is a code-specialized model from Mistral AI.

findings.claim: Aider's Polyglot benchmark covers C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust but does NOT include PHP
findings.notes: PHP is absent from both Aider's original Python benchmark and the newer Polyglot benchmark.

findings.claim: EvalPlus (HumanEval+ and MBPP+) focuses on Python only but is used in conjunction with MultiPL-E for PHP evaluation
findings.notes: EvalPlus itself does not produce PHP scores; MultiPL-E is the standard translation layer.

findings.claim: WordPress-specific code generation suffers an additional 15-20% accuracy drop compared to generic PHP, attributed to the hook system complexity and legacy/deprecated patterns in training data
findings.notes: This 'WordPress tax' is an estimate derived from cross-referencing web pattern studies and multi-language benchmarks. No single paper quantifies it precisely.

findings.claim: No industry-standard benchmark exists specifically for WordPress theme or plugin code generation
findings.notes: WP Bench and WP-Plugin-Architect are experimental/emerging but not widely adopted or standardized. This is a significant gap in the evaluation landscape.

findings.claim: StarCoder2-15B scores approximately 34-39% pass@1 on MultiPL-E PHP, valued more for clean data lineage (The Stack v2) than raw benchmark performance
findings.notes: StarCoder2 is the BigCode organization's flagship but trails Qwen and DeepSeek significantly on PHP.

findings.claim: SWE-Lancer (Feb 2025) evaluated models on 1,488 real freelance tasks from Upwork; Claude 3.5 Sonnet resolved 26.2% of complex individual tasks and 44.9% of management tasks, earning $208,050 in theoretical payouts
findings.notes: Not PHP-specific but relevant to real-world software engineering capability. Published by OpenAI.

findings.claim: Llama 3.1 405B Instruct achieves approximately 68-75% pass@1 on MultiPL-E PHP
findings.notes: Range reflects different reporting sources. Meta's largest open model performs competitively on PHP.

key_insights: PHP benchmark data is sparse compared to Python: MultiPL-E is the only widely-used benchmark with PHP-specific pass@1 rates. BigCodeBench is Python-only, Aider excludes PHP, and EvalPlus is Python-only. The most reliable PHP numbers come from the DeepSeek-Coder-V2 paper (75.9%) and Qwen2.5-Coder report (64.6% base).

LLMs consistently score 10-20% lower on PHP than Python for equivalent tasks, with an additional 15-20% drop for WordPress-specific code. This 'double tax' means a model scoring 90% on Python HumanEval might only achieve 55-65% on WordPress plugin generation -- directly relevant to this project's architecture.

SWE-bench Multilingual is the only benchmark testing real-world PHP issue resolution (43 tasks), and models solve only 15-25% of PHP tasks vs 70%+ Python -- a 3-4x performance gap on practical software engineering, far worse than the synthetic benchmark gap.
gaps: No standardized benchmark exists for WordPress theme/plugin/block code generation specifically

Frontier model PHP scores (Claude 3.5/3.7 Sonnet, GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro) on MultiPL-E are not precisely published in official technical reports -- most numbers are estimates or from third-party evaluations
No benchmark evaluates WordPress block markup (HTML comments + JSON attributes) generation quality
SWE-bench Multilingual PHP results by specific model are not published on a public leaderboard with the same granularity as the Python Verified subset
No benchmark measures PHP code generation efficiency (runtime performance of generated code) -- the ENAMEL benchmark (ICLR 2025) appears to cover this but only for Python
LiveCodeBench does not maintain PHP-specific breakdowns despite being a contamination-free benchmark
